mission.title: Install and Configure Chaosblade on Kubernetes
mission.description: ChaosBlade is a powerful chaos engineering toolkit that helps improve the fault tolerance of distributed systems through various experimental scenarios. Installing it allows you to conduct chaos experiments in your Kubernetes environment to ensure business continuity.

steps.title: Add the ChaosBlade Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official ChaosBlade Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add chaosblade https://charts.chaosblade.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install ChaosBlade using Helm
steps.description:
Install ChaosBlade in your Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and create a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install chaosblade chaosblade/chaosblade --version v1.8.0 --namespace chaosblade --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ChaosBlade pods are running successfully in the chaosblade namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n chaosblade
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the ChaosBlade deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment chaosblade --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n chaosblade
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for ChaosBlade
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow ChaosBlade to access necessary resources.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: chaosblade
  name: chaosblade-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/exec"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete"]
EOF

kubectl create rolebinding chaosblade-role-binding --role=chaosblade-role --serviceaccount=chaosblade:default --namespace=chaosblade
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the ChaosBlade release from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall chaosblade --namespace chaosblade
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with ChaosBlade.
```bash
kubectl delete crd chaosblades.chaosblade.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace created for ChaosBlade to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace chaosblade
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of ChaosBlade for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all -n chaosblade -o yaml > chaosblade-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is updated to get the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ChaosBlade
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the ChaosBlade installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade chaosblade chaosblade/chaosblade --version v1.8.0 --namespace chaosblade
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the upgraded ChaosBlade pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the ChaosBlade pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability and node conditions.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: ChaosBlade CLI not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the CLI commands are not responding, ensure that the service is running and accessible.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: Failed to create chaos experiments
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors while creating chaos experiments, check the logs of the ChaosBlade pod for more information.
```bash
kubectl logs <chaosblade-pod-name> -n chaosblade
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of ChaosBlade will have all pods running in the 'chaosblade' namespace, and you will be able to execute chaos experiments using the CLI. Resource limits and RBAC should be properly configured for optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add chaosblade https://charts.chaosblade.io
helm repo update
helm install chaosblade chaosblade/chaosblade --version v1.8.0 --namespace chaosblade --create-namespace
kubectl set resources deployment chaosblade --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n chaosblade

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: chaosblade
  name: chaosblade-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/exec"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete"]
EOF

kubectl create rolebinding chaosblade-role-binding --role=chaosblade-role --serviceaccount=chaosblade:default --namespace=chaosblade

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed.
